prompt: AI infrastructure, inference, and compute startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, amount, date, valuation if known, one-line, and the SPECIFIC wedge/differentiator. Include inference providers, GPU clouds, serving, orchestration. Cite sources.
content:
**Here are key AI infrastructure/inference/compute startups (focusing on inference providers, GPU clouds, serving, and orchestration) that raised $50M+ in 2025–2026 (as of mid-2026 data).** I prioritized those with explicit large rounds in the period, drawing from funding announcements and trackers. Details are based on reported figures; valuations are post-money where specified. Not exhaustive, as private rounds can be underreported, but these are prominent examples matching the criteria.[[1]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)

- **DeepInfra** (Palo Alto, CA)  
  **Amount/Date**: $107M Series B (May 2026).  
  **Valuation**: Undisclosed.  
  **One-line**: Purpose-built AI inference cloud operating its own GPU infrastructure.  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: Owns and operates GPUs across eight US data centers with OpenAI-compatible APIs supporting 190+ open-source models; high-scale inference (processing ~5T tokens/week) optimized for cost/efficiency vs. general clouds.[[1]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)[[2]](https://aifundingtracker.com/)

- **Inferact** (vLLM team)  
  **Amount/Date**: $150M seed (January 22, 2026).  
  **Valuation**: $800M.  
  **One-line**: Commercialization of the popular open-source vLLM LLM serving engine.  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: vLLM’s efficient PagedAttention and continuous batching for high-throughput, low-latency LLM inference/serving and orchestration at scale.[[3]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/17/here-are-the-17-us-based-ai-companies-that-have-raised-100m-or-more-in-2026/)[[4]](https://cryptorank.io/news/feed/2f47e-inferact-vllm-funding-ai-inference)

- **Runware** (San Francisco)  
  **Amount/Date**: $50M Series A (December 11, 2025).  
  **Valuation**: Undisclosed (total raised ~$66M).  
  **One-line**: AI inference platform with a unified “Sonic Inference Engine.”  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: “One API for all AI” optimizing generative model performance and enabling deployment of millions of Hugging Face models with strong cost/performance gains.[[5]](https://www.crescendo.ai/news/latest-vc-investment-deals-in-ai-startups)

- **Baseten**  
  **Amount/Date**: $300M Series E (January 2026, $5B valuation); prior $150M Series D (September 2025, $2.15B valuation); earlier $75M Series C (February 2025).  
  **Valuation**: Reached $5B post-$300M round (talks of larger later rounds reported).  
  **One-line**: Developer platform for deploying and serving AI models with reliable inference.  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: Focus on performant, reliable, and cost-efficient inference infrastructure tailored for production AI apps/agents (including tools like Baseten Loops for RL).[[6]](https://sacra.com/c/baseten/)[[7]](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/ai-inference-provider-baseten-talks-raise-1-billion-11-billion-valuation)

- **Lambda** (Lambda Labs / Superintelligence Cloud)  
  **Amount/Date**: >$1.5B Series E (November 18, 2025); prior $480M Series D (February 2025).  
  **Valuation**: ~$5.9B post-Series E (earlier ~$2.5B).  
  **One-line**: GPU cloud provider building large-scale AI compute infrastructure.  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: “Superintelligence Cloud” with gigawatt-scale AI factories, broad NVIDIA GPU access, and direct hyperscaler/enterprise deals (including Nvidia renting back capacity).[[8]](https://sacra.com/c/lambda-labs/)[[9]](https://lambda.ai/blog/lambda-raises-over-1.5b-from-twg-global-usit-to-build-superintelligence-cloud-infrastructure)

- **Groq**  
  **Amount/Date**: $750M Series E (September 17, 2025, $6.9B valuation).  
  **Valuation**: $6.9B.  
  **One-line**: AI inference chip (LPU) and cloud provider.  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: Custom Language Processing Units (LPUs) delivering fast, low-cost inference for LLMs vs. general-purpose GPUs, with integrated cloud offering.[[10]](https://groq.com/newsroom/groq-raises-750-million-as-inference-demand-surges)

- **Verda** (formerly DataCrunch, Helsinki)  
  **Amount/Date**: $117M (equity + debt, ~April 2026); prior ~$64M Series A / €55M (2025–early 2026).  
  **Valuation**: Undisclosed.  
  **One-line**: European GPU cloud platform expanding globally.  
  **Specific wedge/differentiator**: Cash-flow-positive GPU cloud focused on high-performance AI training/inference, scaling from Nordics into US/UK/Asia with strong local financing.[[11]](https://thenextweb.com/news/verda-117m-funding-lifeline-ventures-ai-cloud)

**Additional notes**:  
- Other mentions (e.g., Fractile’s ~$220M for inference-specific chips or Fluidstack’s $653M GPU cloud round) appear in trackers but have fewer public specifics on exact 2025–2026 timing/valuations.[[1]](https://aifundingtracker.com/ai-startup-funding-news-today/)  
- Larger players like CoreWeave completed major debt facilities and an IPO in 2025 but are less “startup”-oriented post-listing.  
- Many rounds emphasize inference (the dominant compute bottleneck post-training) and owned/specialized hardware/cloud capacity over general-purpose options. Sources primarily include company announcements, TechCrunch, Sacra, Tracxn, and funding trackers. Private details can evolve quickly.
